title: [InstCombine] Fold fcmp eq over min/max-like select into compare on input
author: Nathiyaa-Sengodan
description:
Fold equality compares against floating min/max-like select patterns.

This handles:
fcmp oeq/ueq (select (fcmp Pred X, K), X, K), C
fcmp oeq/ueq (select (fcmp Pred X, K), K, X), C

when the select is recognized as an fmaxnum/fminnum-like operation and one
operand is the constant bound K. The result can be expressed as a
direct comparison against X based on the relation between C and K:
    
  For max-like patterns:
  
  max(X, K) == C:
  
  C > K  -> X == C
  C == K -> X <= K
  
  For min-like patterns:
  
  min(X, K) == C:
  
  C < K  -> X == C
  C == K -> X >= K

Alive2 - https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/GmSEB2
Fixes  https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/185734

tests.test_body:
define i1 @fold_fcmp_full_clamp_oeq_hi(double %x) {
  %min_cmp = fcmp ogt double %x, 5.000000e+00
  %min = select i1 %min_cmp, double 5.000000e+00, double %x
  %max_cmp = fcmp olt double %min, 2.000000e+00
  %clamp = select nnan i1 %max_cmp, double 2.000000e+00, double %min
  %cmp = fcmp nnan ueq double %clamp, 5.000000e+00
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define i1 @fold_fcmp_full_clamp_mid_const(double %x) {
  %min_cmp = fcmp ogt double %x, 5.000000e+00
  %min = select i1 %min_cmp, double 5.000000e+00, double %x
  %max_cmp = fcmp olt double %min, 2.000000e+00
  %clamp = select i1 %max_cmp, double 2.000000e+00, double %min
  %cmp = fcmp oeq double %clamp, 4.000000e+00
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define i1 @fold_two_clamp_hi(float %arg0, float %arg1) {
  %v0 = fcmp olt float %arg1, 1.000000e+00
  %v1 = select i1 %v0, float %arg1, float 1.000000e+00
  %v2 = fcmp olt float %v1, 0.000000e+00
  %v3 = select i1 %v2, float 0.000000e+00, float %v1
  %v4 = fcmp ogt float %arg0, 2.000000e+00
  %v5 = select i1 %v4, float 2.000000e+00, float %arg0
  %v6 = fcmp olt float %v5, 3.000000e+00
  %v7 = select i1 %v6, float %v5, float 3.000000e+00
  %v8 = fcmp nnan ueq float %v3, 1.000000e+00
  %v9 = fcmp nnan ueq float %v7, 2.000000e+00
  %v10 = and i1 %v8, %v9
  ret i1 %v10
}

define i1 @fold_fcmp_clamp_minmax_alt_oeq_hi(double %x) {
  %max_cmp = fcmp ogt double %x, 2.000000e+00
  %max = select i1 %max_cmp, double %x, double 2.000000e+00
  %min_cmp = fcmp ogt double %max, 5.000000e+00
  %clamp = select i1 %min_cmp, double 5.000000e+00, double %max
  %cmp = fcmp nnan ueq double %clamp, 5.000000e+00
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define <2 x i1> @fold_two_clamp_hi_vec(<2 x float> %arg0, <2 x float> %arg1) {
  %v0 = fcmp ogt <2 x float> %arg1, splat (float 1.000000e+00)
  %v1 = select <2 x i1> %v0, <2 x float> splat (float 1.000000e+00), <2 x float> %arg1
  %v2 = fcmp ogt <2 x float> %v1, zeroinitializer
  %v3 = select <2 x i1> %v2, <2 x float> %v1, <2 x float> zeroinitializer
  %v4 = fcmp olt <2 x float> %arg0, splat (float 5.000000e+00)
  %v5 = select <2 x i1> %v4, <2 x float> %arg0, <2 x float> splat (float 5.000000e+00)
  %v6 = fcmp ogt <2 x float> %v5, splat (float 1.000000e+00)
  %v7 = select <2 x i1> %v6, <2 x float> %v5, <2 x float> splat (float 1.000000e+00)
  %v8 = fcmp oeq <2 x float> %v3, splat (float 1.000000e+00)
  %v9 = fcmp ueq <2 x float> %v7, splat (float 3.000000e+00)
  %v10 = and <2 x i1> %v8, %v9
  ret <2 x i1> %v10
}

define i1 @fold_fcmp_full_clamp_oeq_lo(double %x) {
  %min_cmp = fcmp ogt double %x, 5.000000e+00
  %min = select i1 %min_cmp, double 5.000000e+00, double %x
  %max_cmp = fcmp olt double %min, 2.000000e+00
  %clamp = select i1 %max_cmp, double 2.000000e+00, double %min
  %cmp = fcmp oeq double %clamp, 2.000000e+00
  ret i1 %cmp
}

comments.author: github-actions[bot]
comments.body:
<!--LLVM CODE FORMAT COMMENT: {clang-format}-->

:white_check_mark: With the latest revision this PR passed the C/C++ code formatter.

comments.author: Nathiyaa-Sengodan
comments.body:
> I'd like to decompose this transformation into two parts, each of which eliminates one min/max pattern. For example: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/qDhMki
> 
> ```
> max(min(x, 5.0), 2.0) == 5.0 ->
> min(x, 5.0) == 5.0 ->
> x >= 5.0
> ```

Thanks. 
I have updated the PR, so that we decompose this into two parts, instead of folding the entire clamp pattern. 
Updated the tests also accordingly.
